title: AI-Driven Resume Quality & ATS Analysis System
description: Built an end-to-end AI hiring analysis system that ingested resumes, LinkedIn exports, GitHub metadata, and transcripts into a structured PostgreSQL knowledge graph, then scored candidate-to-job alignment using a custom ATS engine built on sentence-transformers and scikit-learn. Orchestrated a multi-agent LangGraph pipeline across OpenAI and Ollama models to generate explainable gap analyses and targeted resume improvements, with validation logic that grounded all outputs strictly in existing candidate evidence to prevent hallucinated credentials. Delivered the full system through an interactive Textual terminal UI, with reproducible ETL pipelines and documented prompt engineering practices supporting reliable, auditable AI-assisted hiring decisions.

title: California Real Estate Price Prediction
description: Built a machine learning web application that predicts single-family residence prices across California using ensemble models including XGBoost and LightGBM, trained on real estate market data with features such as location, property size, and neighborhood demographics. Deployed an interactive Streamlit dashboard that allows users to input property characteristics and receive price predictions in real time, supported by dynamic visualizations built with Plotly. The project involved custom model training pipelines with scikit-learn preprocessing, hyperparameter tuning, and model serialization via Joblib to serve predictions efficiently within the application.

title: eCommerce Session Review Analysis - Predicting Online Shopper Purchase Intent
description: Built classification models using Random Forest and XGBoost on 12,330 e-commerce browsing sessions to predict purchase intent, applying threshold tuning to optimize recall and reduce missed conversions, achieving ~90% accuracy. Applied K-Means clustering to segment shoppers into five behavioral archetypes, revealing conversion rates ranging from 0.4% for bounce-prone visitors to 24.3% for high-intent consumers. Identified product browsing depth and administrative page engagement as the strongest purchase predictors using Python, scikit-learn, pandas, and seaborn.

title: Dsc207miniproject
description: Built a data science project using Python to collect, process, and visualize structured datasets through HTTP requests and pandas-based data manipulation. Applied statistical analysis and visualization techniques using matplotlib and seaborn to uncover patterns and trends within the data. Demonstrated proficiency in the full data science pipeline, from raw data ingestion to insight generation, within a Jupyter Notebook environment.

title: Sleepercelmanual
description: Built a manual, notebook-based implementation of a sleeper cell detection system using Python, NumPy, and Pandas to process and evaluate network or behavioral data via the Requests library for data retrieval. The project served as a hands-on reference version for understanding the underlying logic and data pipelines before automation, walking through each computational step explicitly. This manual approach allowed for granular inspection of intermediate results and validation of the detection methodology at each stage.

title: SleeperCel
description: Built a full-stack AI roster building assistant for fantasy football that integrates with the public Sleeper API to display user leagues, rosters, and available players in real time. The frontend was developed using React and Vite, while the backend leveraged Python with pandas and NumPy to process player statistics and support data-driven roster recommendations. The project combined live API data fetching with statistical analysis to provide actionable lineup decisions for fantasy football managers.

title: Bike Traffic in Cambridge Interactive Visualization
description: Built an interactive visualization of bike traffic patterns across Cambridge using JavaScript, CSS, and HTML to display station-level ridership data on a dynamic map. Implemented filtering controls and visual encodings such as scaled markers and color gradients to communicate traffic volume and flow direction across different times of day. The project enabled users to identify high-traffic corridors and temporal usage trends across the city's bike network.

title: Desktop Tutorial
description: This tutorial project walked through the foundational workflow of GitHub Desktop, demonstrating how to initialize a repository, edit files locally, and sync changes with a remote repository. The project used a simple README file as a hands-on exercise to practice committing and pushing modifications through GitHub's desktop client. The exercise established core version control habits, including staging changes and writing commit messages, essential for managing collaborative and personal coding projects.

title: How Airbnb Tourism was impacted by the San Diego Sunshine
description: A static graph which explores Airbnb bookings and revenue throughout the year in San Diego, California. The graph shows how the number of bookings and revenue changes throughout the year, with the highest peaks in the summer months.

title: Study on the Impact of Estrus on Female Rats Throughout the Day
description: This graph is a static visualization of the body temperature across male and female mice. Used to explore differences in the daily cylce of body temperature while highlighting the impact of female estrus.

title: AllRecipes Rating Predictive Classification Model
description: This machine learning model predicts the ratings of recipes on AllRecipes.com based on features of the recipe. We created a multiclass-classifier using a RandomForest.

title: Study of the Presence of Educational Institutions and Income Throughout California
description: An exploratory data analysis of the presence of educational institutions and income throughout California. The project uses a heatmap to show the distribution of educational institutions and income throughout the state of California.

title: Image Processor
description: Built an image processor that can read in an image, apply a filter to it, and then output the processed image. The image processor can apply filters such as negate, grayscale, and edge detection. This project was built using Python.

title: Asteroid Landfall Simulation
description: Written using pandas in the Jupyter Notebook environment, this project simulates the landfall of asteroids using historical datasets, probabilistic modeling, and bootstrapping.

title: Interactive Visualization of Mice Body Temperature Across Gender
description: An interactive visualization of the body temperature across the gender of mice. The visualization allows users to explore the differences in body temperature while allowing filtering and the altering of scope. Features a tooltip which highlights Mouse ID allowing us to examine indvidual mice along with their estrus cycle( should they be female).
